3	description: Per-file bidirectional verifier. Reads one .lean file and its blueprint chapter; reports (a) whether the Lean file follows the blueprint — fake/placeholder statements, signature mismatches with \lean{...} hints, proof divergence, "temporary" defs — AND (b) whether the blueprint chapter is detailed enough to have guided a faithful formalization of this file.
4	write_domain: "task_results/**"
5	read_only: true
6	can_spawn: false
7	default_enabled: false
8	mandatory: [review]
9	dispatcher_notes: |
10	  - I am highly recommended in the review phase. Dispatch one checker
11	    per `.lean` file that received prover work this iteration. Each
12	    dispatch is independent and parallel-able.
13	
14	    **You may skip me this iter when NO `.lean` file received prover
15	    work** (e.g. the prover phase was skipped, or every prover
16	    INCOMPLETE'd without committing edits). Record under `## Subagent
17	    skips` in `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`:
18	    ``- lean-vs-blueprint-checker: no .lean files modified this iter
19	    (no prover edits to verify)``.
20	
21	    Do NOT skip a per-file dispatch when the prover DID commit edits
22	    to that file — skipping a prover-touched file is the review-phase
23	    failure this affordance exists to surface, not to enable. The
24	    "highly recommended" framing applies to the whole-iter decision,
25	    not to picking-and-choosing within an iter that has multiple
26	    prover-touched files.
27	  - Also dispatch on any file a code-audit subagent in your catalog
28	    flagged as suspicious, even if it received no prover work this
29	    iter.
30	  - The directive must name exactly one Lean file and its blueprint
31	    chapter — no global context, no strategy snapshot. My value is
32	    the narrow file-vs-chapter view.
33	  - I report bidirectionally: Lean → blueprint AND blueprint → Lean.
34	    The blueprint can be the failure (too thin to guide
35	    formalization, missing the level of detail the Lean code clearly
36	    needed), not only the Lean. If I flag a chapter as inadequate,
37	    the plan agent should dispatch the catalog's blueprint-writing
38	    subagent to address it.
39	  - Any must-fix-this-iter finding I report blocks downstream work on

42	---
43	
44	# Lean ↔ Blueprint Checker
45	
46	You compare **one Lean file** against **its blueprint chapter** and report whether the Lean follows the blueprint faithfully. You are read-only.
47	
48	## Your Job
49	
50	Your directive names one `.lean` file (e.g. `Algebra/WLocal.lean`) and its corresponding blueprint chapter (e.g. `blueprint/src/chapters/Algebra_WLocal.tex`). You audit **bidirectionally**:
51	
52	### (A) Does the Lean follow the blueprint?
53	
54	For each `\lean{...}`-tagged block in the chapter, verify the Lean side:
55	
56	- **Declaration exists** with the right name (matches `\lean{namespace.theorem_name}`).
57	- **Signature matches** the informal statement in the chapter — the types are what the prose says they should be. A statement that type-checks but uses the wrong type class (e.g. `Smooth f` where the prose says "smooth of relative dimension n") is **wrong**, not "approximate".
58	- **Proof follows the blueprint sketch** — the proof in the Lean file should be a formalization of the steps in the chapter's `\begin{proof}...\end{proof}`, not a different argument that happens to produce the same conclusion.
59	- **No fake / placeholder statements** — definitions or theorems whose body is just `:= sorry` or a trivially-true tautology while the chapter prose claims something substantive.
60	- **No "temporary" definitions left in place** — comments like `-- TODO: replace with the real definition`, `-- placeholder until we figure out X`, or definitions whose body is suspicious (e.g. `def Foo := True`, weakened-wrong definitions standing in for the real concept).
61	- **No comments that explain away wrong code** — "we are using a wrong definition for now", "this proof is incomplete but will be fixed", etc. These are red flags, not acceptable workflow notes.
62	
63	### (B) Is the blueprint adequate for this Lean file?
64	
65	The blueprint can also be the failure mode. For the chapter you're reading, check:
66	
67	- **Every Lean declaration in this file should map to a blueprint block** (via `\lean{...}`) OR be an explicitly-helper-only declaration. If the file has substantive declarations the blueprint doesn't reference at all, the chapter is incomplete.
68	- **Proof sketches in the blueprint should be detailed enough** for a prover to formalize correctly. If you see Lean proofs that clearly went through significant reasoning the blueprint's `\begin{proof}` does NOT preview, flag the chapter as under-specified.
69	- **`\lean{...}` hints should pin the Lean target precisely.** If the blueprint says "smooth morphism" without specifying which Mathlib predicate (`Smooth` vs `SmoothOfRelativeDimension n` vs `IsSmooth`), and the Lean file ended up with the wrong one, that's BOTH a Lean error AND a blueprint clarity failure. Flag both sides.
70	- **Definitions in the blueprint should be at the right level of generality** for the Lean to consume. If the blueprint defines something narrower than the project actually needs and the Lean had to write a parallel API to cover the gap, that's a blueprint adequacy failure.
71	
72	Both directions matter. Don't excuse a wrong Lean statement by saying "the blueprint is loose"; don't excuse a thin blueprint by saying "the Lean works anyway." Report both.
73	
74	## Directive Format
75	
76	```markdown
77	# Lean ↔ Blueprint Checker Directive
78	
79	## Slug
80	<slug>
81	
82	## Lean file
83	<path/to/file.lean>
84	
85	## Blueprint chapter
86	blueprint/src/chapters/<slug>.tex
87	
88	## Known issues
89	<things the review agent already knows and doesn't want re-reported>
90	```
91	
92	That's it. No strategy snapshot, no references, no full project context — just the two files and any pre-known issues to skip. Cross-file consistency is the job of the code-audit and blueprint-review subagents in the catalog; you stay focused on this one pair.
93	
94	## What you do
95	
96	1. Read your directive.
97	2. Read the Lean file completely. Note every declaration (`def`, `theorem`, `lemma`, `instance`, `axiom`, …) and its signature.
98	3. Read the blueprint chapter completely. List every `\lean{...}` reference and its corresponding declaration block.
99	4. **For each `\lean{...}` block**: locate the Lean declaration. Verify:
100	   - It exists.

### **NEW TRAP — middle-object-type-mismatch in `≫` chains**

Diagnostic signal: `rw`/`simp` silently refuses to match a Mathlib lemma's LHS even though the pattern text appears verbatim in the goal. Root cause: the surrounding `CategoryStruct.comp`'s middle-object type is defeq-but-not-syntactically-equal between goal and lemma. Fix: `change ... ≫ ((X : A ⟶ B) ≫ Y) = _` to force the middle-object type explicitly. Same family of trap as iter-176's `Fin.isValue` simp recipe and iter-175 chart-bridge-prover-bypass. (Detailed write-up will land in PROJECT_STATUS.md Knowledge Base.)

### **`Algebra.compHom`-based instances are a heartbeat sink under `erw`**

`erw [pullbackSpecIso_hom_base]` against a goal carrying `Spec.map (algMap kbar (Away_i ⊗ GmRing))` deterministic-times-out at 200k heartbeats during `isDefEq`. The instance synthesis for `Algebra` on `TensorProduct kbar Away_i GmRing` is the heartbeat sink — `algebraKbarAway` is `Algebra.compHom`-based, which the elaborator struggles to unfold under `erw`'s defeq-permissive unification. Do NOT escalate `rw` → `erw` for these patterns; either rewrite via a project-side wrapper that bypasses instance synthesis, or thread the algebra map explicitly.

### **Consult-driven refactors are reliable when wrapped in `refactor` (not `prover`)**

Both `cover-bridge-uniform-i` and `relative-spec-block-a` landed verbatim per their analogist recipes on first attempt. The `refactor` subagent's blocking semantics + structural-edit scope guarantee that the prover lanes consuming the refactor outputs see the post-refactor file state. iter-179 had 2 such refactor lanes co-dispatched without race (each touched disjoint files).

222	## Knowledge Base
223	
224	### Proof Patterns (reusable across targets)
225	
226	- **`IsDiscreteValuationRing.TFAE.out 0 4` is the canonical Mathlib-aligned recipe for proving `IsDiscreteValuationRing R` on a Noetherian local domain via principal-max-ideal** *(NEW iter-178 Archon canonical — `Scheme.localRing_dvr_of_codim_one` main body landed kernel-clean via this recipe.)* When the goal is `IsDiscreteValuationRing R` on a Noetherian local domain `R` (e.g. a stalk of an integral scheme at a codim-1 point), the recipe is: (1) establish `[IsLocalRing R]`, `[IsNoetherianRing R]`, `[IsDomain R]`, `¬ IsField R` (the last via `IsLocalRing.isField_iff_maximalIdeal_eq` applied to a `maximalIdeal ≠ ⊥` witness); (2) `have tfae := IsDiscreteValuationRing.TFAE R hfield`; (3) `exact (tfae.out 0 4).mpr ⟨principal, ne_bot⟩`. The substantive geometric content reduces to proving `Submodule.IsPrincipal (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) ∧ maximalIdeal R ≠ ⊥` — the cleanest factoring for Mathlib-gap deferral (e.g. when the bridge `Smooth → IsRegularLocalRing` is still pending Stacks 00TT/00TX). On a smooth scheme, the typeclass scaffold auto-derives: `IsLocallyNoetherian X` via `LocallyOfFiniteType.isLocallyNoetherian X.hom`; `IsNoetherianRing` of stalk via `instIsNoetherianRingCarrierStalkCommRingCatPresheafOfIsLocallyNoetherian`; `IsLocalRing` of stalk via `LocallyRingedSpace.instIsLocalRingCarrierStalkCommRingCatPresheaf`; `IsDomain` of stalk via `instIsDomainCarrierStalkCommRingCatPresheafOfIsIntegral`. Reusable for any "DVR-at-prime-divisor" / "DVR-at-codim-1-point" Mathlib-aligned proof on Noetherian schemes.
227	
228	- **Symptom-based case-split on `∀ Y, order Y f = 0` is cleaner than algebra-tower `∃ c, algebraMap c = f` case-split for "constant vs non-constant rational function" reasoning** *(NEW iter-178 Archon canonical — `Scheme.WeilDivisor.principal_degree_zero` constant branch landed axiom-clean via this recipe.)* When a proof needs to case-split on "f is constant vs non-constant" for `f : C.functionField`, do NOT reach for `by_cases hex : ∃ c : kbar, algebraMap kbar C.functionField c = f`. Direct `Algebra kbar C.functionField` synthesis FAILS — `Spec (CommRingCat.of kbar)`'s function-field algebra instance needs `[IsDomain kbar]` registered AND `C.hom : C.left ⟶ Spec (.of kbar)` does NOT auto-propagate algebra towers downstream; the manual construction is ~40-60 LOC of substrate work. Instead, case-split on the SYMPTOM: `by_cases hconst : ∀ Y : C.left.PrimeDivisor, Scheme.RationalMap.order Y f = 0`. The constant branch ("all orders vanish") closes via `Finsupp.ext + change Scheme.RationalMap.order Y f = 0 + Finsupp.sum_zero_index` for degree-zero claims. **Reusable** for any "constant-vs-non-constant rational function" reasoning where the algebra-tower setup is incidental rather than load-bearing.
229	
230	- **`Scheme.Modules.Hom.isIso_iff_isIso_app` is the canonical Mathlib hook for section-wise iso of `Scheme.Modules` morphisms — factor substantive content into a named helper carrying a SUBSTANTIVE `IsIso` type** *(NEW iter-178 Archon canonical — `canonicalBaseChangeMap_isIso` body shape; gold-standard non-laundering pattern; CONFIRMS the iter-177 KB entry on "helper-with-sorry as honest scaffold".)* When the goal is `IsIso φ` for a `Scheme.Modules` morphism `φ`, the Mathlib idiom is `rw [Scheme.Modules.Hom.isIso_iff_isIso_app]; intro U; exact helper sq F U` (located at `Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Modules/Sheaf.lean`). Factor the section-level claim into a named helper `theorem foo_app_app_isIso ... : IsIso ((φ.app F).app U) := sorry`. The helper TYPE must be SUBSTANTIVE (the actual `IsIso` claim, not a tautology) — this distinguishes the pattern from iter-176 RelativeSpec laundering (placeholder bodies `:= X` / `:= 𝟙 X`). Blueprint readers can see exactly which Stacks tag is the remaining work item. Reusable for any "canonical map of `Scheme.Modules` is iso" claim where the canonical map is constructible kernel-clean.
231	
232	- **`CategoryTheory.projectiveDimension (ModuleCat.of R M) : WithBot ℕ∞` is the Mathlib alias for `Module.projectiveDimension R M`** *(NEW iter-178 Archon canonical — `Module.projectiveDimension`, axiom-clean kernel-only one-liner.)* When the project needs `Module.projectiveDimension R M` and the file already `open CategoryTheory`s, the 1-line body is `CategoryTheory.projectiveDimension (ModuleCat.of R _M)`. Located at `Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Abelian/Projective/Dimension.lean:265` with signature `(X : C) [Abelian C] → WithBot ℕ∞`; for `C = ModuleCat R` the `[Abelian _]` instance auto-resolves. No `noncomputable instance` ambient blanket needed beyond the per-decl `noncomputable def` keyword. Reusable for project-side `Module.projectiveDimension`, `Module.injectiveDimension`, `Module.flatDimension` 1-line re-exports.
233	
234	- **`Scheme.RationalMap.mem_domain` swap-pair `Iff.intro` closes "mem_domain ↔ ∃ partial-map with equality" identities in ~3 LOC** *(NEW iter-178 Archon canonical — `Scheme.RationalMap.extend_iff_order_nonneg`, axiom-clean.)* When the goal is `W.point ∈ f.domain ↔ ∃ g : ..., g.toRationalMap = f ∧ W.point ∈ (g.domain : Set _)` (or any reordering of the conjuncts), `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Birational.RationalMap.Scheme.RationalMap.mem_domain` provides `x ∈ f.domain ↔ ∃ g, x ∈ g.domain ∧ g.toRationalMap = f`; project signatures often reorder the conjuncts. The 3-LOC fix: `rw [Scheme.RationalMap.mem_domain]; exact ⟨fun ⟨g, hxg, hg⟩ => ⟨g, hg, hxg⟩, fun ⟨g, hg, hxg⟩ => ⟨g, hxg, hg⟩⟩`. Reusable trap-saver for any "predicate ↔ ∃-quantifier with conjuncts in different order" identity.
235	
236	- **`haveI : IsScalarTower kbar (𝒜 0) (MvPoly ...) := ⟨...⟩` defaults to `IsScalarTower A A A` without explicit `(R := ) (S := ) (A := )` annotations — `pointOfVec` template body does NOT elaborate without them** *(NEW iter-178 Archon canonical — surfaced by Lane 5 `morphismToP1OfGlobalSections` body attempt; 3-LOC cost per `pointOfVec`-template body.)* When a body needs `IsScalarTower kbar (𝒜 0) (MvPoly _ kbar)` for a `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections`-style section-condition proof, the elaborator's metavariable resolution defaults to `IsScalarTower A A A` (a tautology) unless `(R := kbar) (S := ↥(...)) input.new_string:
### Proof Patterns (reusable across targets)

- **Middle-object-type-mismatch in `≫` chains — `change ... ≫ ((X : A ⟶ B) ≫ Y) = _` to retype before `rw`/`simp`** *(NEW iter-179 Archon canonical — surfaced by Lane A `gmScalingP1_chart_PLB_eq` Step C; load-bearing trick that unblocked 4 of 6 recipe steps.)* When `rw [lemma]` or `simp [lemma]` reports "Did not find an occurrence of the pattern" while the pattern text appears verbatim in the goal, the prime suspect is a defeq-but-not-syntactically-equal mismatch on the surrounding `CategoryStruct.comp`'s middle-object type between goal and lemma. Diagnostic signal: pattern text matches; rewrite refuses. Fix: explicit `change EXPR_WITH_TYPED_MIDDLE_OBJECT = _` ascribing the middle-object's type with the lemma's expected form. Worked example (iter-179): `change (gmScalingP1_cover_X_iso kbar i).hom ≫ _ ≫ ((Proj.awayι (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) (MvPolynomial.X i) … Nat.one_pos : Spec _ ⟶ Proj (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)) ≫ (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom) = _` retyped the middle object to `Proj 𝒜` matching `awayι_comp_PLB_hom`'s LHS; the `rw` then fired. Same family of trap as iter-176 `Fin.isValue` simp recipe and iter-175 chart-bridge-prover-bypass. Reusable for any project- or Mathlib-side rewrite that fails with the "pattern verbatim but not found" symptom in a `≫` chain. **Compounding gotcha**: when there are 3+ layered `≫`s and reshaping all middle-objects with explicit types blows up the `change` term to >2KB, the workaround stalls under elaboration timeout — at that point pivot to a structural fix (define a project-side wrapper that takes the chain's middle morphism as an explicit hypothesis, or generalise the consumed lemma's `m`/`hm` arguments to make the natural goal shape match — cf. iter-179 `awayι_comp_PLB_hom` generalisation to `{m : ℕ} (hm : 0 < m)`).

- **`IsDiscreteValuationRing.TFAE.out 0 4` is the canonical Mathlib-aligned recipe

# iter-180 Plan-Agent Recommendations

## Top priority (iter-180 MUST-action)

### REC-1 — Fire iter-181 RETIRE-OR-ESCALATE EARLY at iter-180

**Why**: Lane A's PARTIAL outcome IS the reversal trigger condition the lane directive armed: "if the recipe sticks even on Step 3 (1) after the refactor lands cleanly, the iter-180 plan-agent fires the iter-181 escalation EARLY (do not silently iter another helper-cycle)". The plan-phase refactor `cover-bridge-uniform-i` landed cleanly (no reversal of its own); Lane A's prover got 4 of 6 chart_PLB_eq recipe steps onto file; `pullbackSpecIso_hom_base` does not fire under `Algebra.compHom` instance synthesis (rw silent-refuses; erw 200k heartbeat timeout). 2 iter-177 TEMP axioms persist.

**Concrete options for iter-180 plan-agent**:

1. **Project-side wrapper `pullbackSpecIso_hom_base'`** that takes the algebra map as an explicit hypothesis, bypassing `Algebra.compHom` instance synthesis (~10-15 LOC project-local). LOWEST-RISK, LOWEST-HORIZON.
2. **TO_USER.md escalation** asking the mathematician/Mathlib maintainer about known Algebra.compHom + pullbackSpecIso_hom_base interactions; possible workarounds; whether a Mathlib upstream fix is in flight.
3. **Mathlib upstream PR** strengthening `pullbackSpecIso_hom_base`'s elaboration robustness (~3-week horizon; permanent fix).
4. **Switch to Route C valuative-criterion** alternative for `ℙ¹→A const` per `analogies/gmscaling-cover-bridge.md` Decision 4. Was deemed "no savings" earlier; with 8+ iters of chart-bridge failure history, re-evaluate.

**Recommendation**: pursue (1) — fastest unblock, least architectural risk. (2) in parallel as a heads-up to the mathematician. Defer (3) and (4) until (1) is exhausted.

### REC-2 — Open `gm_grpObj` prover lane consuming `analogies/gm-grpobj-representable.md` recipe

**Why**: iter-179 plan-phase landed the 8-step recipe in the persistent file. Per iter-179 plan Decision 4, "iter-180 commitment: fire `gm_grpObj` body lane consuming this recipe". The route is well-understood (`GrpObj.ofRepresentableBy` + 3-step Yoneda chain, ~75-115 LOC). Fallback path (direct `GrpObj.mk` ~150-200 LOC) is recorded.

**Concrete plan-agent action**: dispatch one prover lane on `AlgebraicJacobian/Genus0BaseObjects/Points.lean:251 gm_grpObj`. Lane directive must quote the 8-step recipe verbatim and the fallback trigger condition (universe issues / IsOver defeq / IsScalarTower stall ≥2 iters).

### REC-3 — Address auditor's must-fix items 3 + 4 (type-level sorries on `LineBundlePullback.OnProduct` + `FGAPicRepresentability.{picSharp, divFunctor}`)

**Why**: lean-auditor `iter179-touched` (`task_results/lean-auditor-iter179-touched.md` L403-405) flagged the project's longest-standing structural debt: 3 type-level `sorry`s that taint downstream pins in 2 files. These were not iter-179 targets, but they are MUST-FIX SEVERITY per the auditor's standing rule.

**Path-forward (iter-180+)**: per the planner's prior decisions, A.1.b (PicSharp on-product gate) is blocked on this exact debt. The recommended sequencing is (a) wait for `RelativeSpec` body work to fully retire (iter-179 closed UniversalProperty + affine_base_iff; helpers + base_change remain); then (b) dispatch a mathlib-analogist consult on `Scheme.Modules.IsInvertible`-style typeclass that the `OnProduct` carrier needs. NOT iter-180-actionable on its own without consult; track in `task_pending.md`.

## Medium priority

### REC-4 — Re-open OCofP + RRFormula body lanes (deferred iter-179)

**Why**: progress-critic `route179` flagged both as STUCK-by-inaction; iter-179 deferred them to capacity. Each contains body sorries that are smaller individually (Hartshorne II.7.7 cohomological vanishing for `globalSections_iff`; RR for genus 0 for `l_eq_degree_plus_one_of_genus_zero`).

**Concrete plan-agent action**: per iter-179 plan Decision 3, iter-180 should "pick the *one* sorry across these 8 with the cleanest Mathlib-idiom recipe". Suggested first pick: `RRFormula.l_eq_degree_plus_one_of_genus_zero` (RR for genus 0 is smaller than the SES-additivity dim-count).

### REC-5 — Dispatch mathlib-analogist consults on the 2 Mathlib gaps gating Thm32 helper and CodimOne helper

**Why**: `task_results/Albanese_Thm32RationalMapExtension.lean.md` L120-127 names `Scheme.Smooth.isReduced` (Stacks 04QM / 01V8) as the Mathlib gap that, once closed, retires both Thm32's `av_isIntegral_and_codimOneFree` helper sorry AND directly improves the CodimOne `smooth_codim_one_…` helper.

`task_results/Albanese_CodimOneExtension.lean.md` L186-201 names a second gap: `Scheme.IsSmooth.isRegularLocalRing_stalk` (Stacks 00TT / 01V3) + `Scheme.ringKrullDim_stalk_of_coheight` (coheight ⟹ ringKrullDim).

**Concrete plan-agent action**: dispatch one mathlib-analogist consult `smooth-stalk-regular` (api-alignment mode) on whether Mathlib b80f227 ships either gap-fill OR whether the project should build a local instance per the recipe in `task_results/Albanese_CodimOneExtension.lean.md`. Output persists at `analogies/smooth-stalk-regular.md`.

### REC-6 — Iter-180+ Lane B follow-up: discharge the 2 RelativeSpec named helpers

**Why**: Lane B closed the file's main downstream theorems but left 2 named helper sorries (`Scheme.QcohAlgebra.pullback.coequifibered`, `Scheme.RelativeSpec.pullback_iso`). Per `task_results/Picard_RelativeSpec.lean.md` Next steps L129-152, both are well-decomposed but each needs ~30-50 LOC of substantive Mathlib-gap work:
- `coequifibered` via `MorphismProperty.pullback_fst` applied to UniversalProperty + `IsAffineOpen.isLocalization_basicOpen`.
- `pullback_iso` via the colimit cocone universal property of `Cover.RelativeGluingData.glued`.

Either can be a dedicated iter-180+ lane. Pin `coequifibered` first (smaller and cleaner Mathlib idiom).

## Soft notes / housekeeping

### REC-7 — Investigate auditor's "possible dead `have` in RelativeSpec L322" (verify-only)

**Why**: lean-auditor `iter179-touched` (`task_results/lean-auditor-iter179-touched.md` L411) flagged `have h : IsAffineHom (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜) := UniversalProperty 𝒜` is bound but never explicitly used in the next line `exact isAffine_of_isAffineHom (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)`. ### REC-8 — Code-style: `exact sorry` → `:= sorry` in `RelPicFunctor.lean:235`

**Why**: auditor `iter179-touched` (Major L412). Stylistic only — semantically identical. 1-LOC fix.

**Why**: auditor flagged 3 stale comment blocks that are non-misleading but could be retired:

## Do NOT retry

### NO-RETRY-1 — `pullbackSpecIso_hom_base` via `rw` / `erw` against an `Algebra.compHom`-based Algebra instance on `TensorProduct kbar _ _`

iter-179 verified: 7 attempts failed (rw silent-refuse, fin_cases + Matrix.cons_val_zero/one, slice_lhs, erw timeout, Iso.eq_inv_comp). The blocker is the instance synthesis pathway, not the pattern unification. Future Lane A retries MUST add a structural workaround (REC-1 option 1) BEFORE attempting `rw`/`erw` again.

`task_results/Albanese_CodimOneExtension.lean.md` L17-21: Path D1 (tighten signature with `Scheme.RationalMap.order`) requires substantive new pullback machinery `RationalMap → function-field ring map` that does NOT ship in Mathlib at `b80f227` and exceeds the 30-LOC discharge cap. Path D2 (chosen iter-179) is the honest fallback. iter-180+ should EITHER (a) wait for Mathlib pullback machinery to land, or (b) add a substantive `extend_iff_pullback_order_nonneg` AS A NEW LEMMA (not by retrying D1 on the renamed lemma).

### NO-RETRY-3 — RatCurveIso Pin 2 (`morphism_degree_via_pole_divisor`) without Hartshorne II.6.9 multiplicativity-of-degree

Pin 2 is gated on Hartshorne II.6.9 multiplicativity-of-degree under finite pullback (Mathlib gap; see `task_pending.md` L116-117). Do not dispatch a body lane on Pin 2 until that infrastructure is in place OR a substantive workaround analogist consult lands.

Per Lane C task result + the chapter pin's downstream gating. iter-180+ needs an architectural argument or signature shift; helper-multiplication will not close.

- **Path D2 honest demotion** is a viable corrective for auditor "shallow body + docstring overclaim" findings when the substantive content is bounded by Mathlib infrastructure gaps.

1	# Lean ↔ Blueprint Check Report
2	
3	## Slug
4	relativespec-iter179
5	
6	## Iteration
7	179
8	
9	## Files audited
10	- Lean: `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/RelativeSpec.lean`
11	- Blueprint: `blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_RelativeSpec.tex`
12	
13	## Per-declaration
14	
15	### `\lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.QcohAlgebra}` (chapter: `def:qc_sheaf_of_algebras`)
16	- **Lean target exists**: yes — `structure QcohAlgebra (X : Scheme.{u})` at L143–158.
17	- **Signature matches**: partial. Blueprint prose pins "sheaf of `O_X`-algebras whose underlying `O_X`-module is quasi-coherent". Lean encodes this as a 3-field structure: (i) `sheaf : TopCat.Sheaf CommRingCat.{u} X.toPresheafedSpace`, (ii) `unit : X.sheaf ⟶ sheaf`, (iii) `coequifibered : NatTrans.Coequifibered (Functor.whiskerLeft (AffineZariskiSite.toOpensFunctor X).op unit.hom)`. The Stacks-01LL `Coequifibered` predicate is strictly weaker than the full `SheafOfModules.IsQuasicoherent` claimed by the prose, but the Lean docstring (L137–142) acknowledges they are equivalent under `AffineZariskiSite.sheafEquiv`. The encoding is now genuinely non-tautological (iter-179 Block A upgrade — was 2-field iter-174).
18	- **Proof follows sketch**: N/A (definition).
19	- **notes**: Field (iii) is the iter-179 Block A landing referenced in the directive. The associativity / commutativity / unit-law content of the prose is enforced by `sheaf` being valued in `CommRingCat`, so the field set is structurally sound.
20	
21	### `\lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RelativeSpec}` (chapter: `thm:relative_spec_exists`)
22	- **Lean target exists**: yes — `noncomputable def RelativeSpec` at L192–193.
23	- **Signature matches**: partial. Body is `(AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData 𝒜.coequifibered).glued`, a substantive Mathlib value — no longer the iter-176 placeholder `:= X`. The Lean defines only the scheme; the structure morphism is a separate `RelativeSpec.structureMorphism`. The blueprint statement also asserts the existence of `π` and the two affine-open compatibility clauses; the Lean defers `π` to the auxiliary `structureMorphism` and the two compatibility clauses to the `relativeGluingData` machinery (not surfaced as separately-named theorems).
24	- **Proof follows sketch**: yes — the construction matches the blueprint's "glue affine pieces `Spec(𝒜(U))` via quasi-coherence transition isomorphisms" sketch via the Mathlib `Cover.RelativeGluingData` infrastructure (`AffineZariskiSite.directedCover`).
25	- **notes**: This iter's body change resolves the iter-176 CRITICAL "weakened-wrong" finding flagged in the directive.
26	
27	### `\lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RelativeSpec.UniversalProperty}` (chapter: `thm:relative_spec_univ`)
28	- **Lean target exists**: yes — `theorem UniversalProperty` at L269–294.
29	- **Signature matches**: **no**. Blueprint prose pins the natural bijection `Hom_X(T, Spec_X(𝒜)) ≅ Hom_{O_X-alg}(𝒜, g_* O_T)` (representability of the Stacks 01LQ functor `F`). Lean encodes only the structural consequence `IsAffineHom (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)`. The blueprint's own iter-173 `% NOTE` (L160–164) explicitly acknowledges this gap and pins iter-174+ as the upgrade horizon.
30	- **Proof follows sketch**: partial — the Lean closes the weaker affineness claim using `isAffineHom_of_forall_exists_isAffineOpen` + `relativeGluingData.toBase_preimage_eq_opensRange_ι` + `isAffineOpen_opensRange`. This is a genuine proof (not `inferInstance` against an identity placeholder), but it does not formalize the Zariski-sheaf / open-subfunctor argument written in the blueprint's `\begin{proof}` (L228–239).
31	- **notes**: Iter-179 Lane B closed this kernel-clean (per directive). Body is now substantive and downstream-consumable, but at a strictly weaker type than the prose pins.
32	
33	### `\lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RelativeSpec.affine_base_iff}` (chapter: `thm:relative_spec_affine_base`)
34	- **Lean target exists**: yes — `theorem affine_base_iff` at L316–323.
35	- **Signature matches**: **no**. Blueprint prose pins the canonical iso `Spec_X(𝒜) ≅ Spec(Γ(X, 𝒜))`. Lean encodes the weaker `IsAffine ((Spec R).RelativeSpec 𝒜)`. The Lean name `affine_base_iff` is misleading — the encoded type is not an iff. Blueprint's iter-173 `% NOTE` (L249–254) acknowledges both gaps.
36	- **Proof follows sketch**: partial — Lean closes via `isAffine_of_isAffineHom` chained off `UniversalProperty`. This is a real "affine over affine is affine" step, but it does not formalize the explicit `f_univ : Spec(A) → Spec(R)` + `φ_univ` representability argument written in the blueprint's `\begin{proof}` (L304–323).
37	- **notes**: Iter-179 Lane B closed this kernel-clean. The iter-176 `% NOTE` on the proof block (L295–303) says the proof "reduces to `change IsAffine (Spec R); inferInstance`" — that is **now stale**; the current proof goes through `UniversalProperty` + `isAffine_of_isAffineHom`.
38	
39	### `\lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RelativeSpec.base_change}` (chapter: `thm:relative_spec_base_change`)
40	- **Lean target exists**: yes — `theorem base_change` at L367–371.
41	- **Signature matches**: **no**. Blueprint prose pins the canonical iso `T ×_X Spec_X(𝒜) ≅ Spec_T(g^* 𝒜)` with the explicit pullback algebra `g^* 𝒜` named. Lean encodes the weaker existential `∃ 𝒜', Nonempty (pullback g (structureMorphism 𝒜) ≅ T.RelativeSpec 𝒜')`. Blueprint's iter-173 `% NOTE` (L333–337) acknowledges this gap.
42	- **Proof follows sketch**: partial — Lean witnesses with the helpers `QcohAlgebra.pullback g _𝒜` and `pullback_iso g _𝒜`. The `base_change` term itself is a 1-line existential tuple, but **the substantive content is delegated to two helpers that each carry a `sorry`** (see Red flags below). So while `base_change` is technically kernel-clean as a term, it is a substantive placeholder via delegation.

77	\section{The relative-spectrum scheme}
78	\label{sec:relspec_scheme}
79	
80	The construction is given affine-locally: on every affine open $U = \Spec R \subseteq X$ one
81	sets $\pi^{-1}(U) := \Spec(\mathcal{A}(U))$ regarded as an $R$-algebra, and the local pieces
82	glue compatibly because $\mathcal{A}$ is quasi-coherent (the transition isomorphism
83	$\mathcal{A}(U) \otimes_R S \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathcal{A}(V)$ for an inclusion
84	$V = \Spec S \subseteq U$ gives an open immersion of the corresponding Specs).
85	
86	\begin{theorem}\leanok
87	[Relative spectrum exists]
88	  \label{thm:relative_spec_exists}
89	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RelativeSpec}
90	  \uses{def:qc_sheaf_of_algebras}
91	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], tag 01LQ (lemma-glue-relative-spec)
92	  % (read from references/stacks-constructions.tex, L381-L405).
93	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
94	  % "In Situation \ref{situation-relative-spec}.
95	  %  There exists a morphism of schemes
96	  %  $$
97	  %  \pi : \underline{\Spec}_S(\mathcal{A}) \longrightarrow S
98	  %  $$
99	  %  with the following properties:
100	  %  \begin{enumerate}
101	  %  \item for every affine open $U \subset S$ there exists an isomorphism
102	  %  $i_U : \pi^{-1}(U) \to \Spec(\mathcal{A}(U))$ over $U$, and
103	  %  \item for $U \subset U' \subset S$ affine open the composition
104	  %  $$
105	  %  \xymatrix{
106	  %  \Spec(\mathcal{A}(U)) \ar[r]^{i_U^{-1}} &
107	  %  \pi^{-1}(U) \ar[rr]^{inclusion} & &
108	  %  \pi^{-1}(U') \ar[r]^{i_{U'}} &
109	  %  \Spec(\mathcal{A}(U'))
110	  %  }
111	  %  $$
112	  %  is the open immersion of Lemma \ref{lemma-spec-inclusion} above.
113	  %  \end{enumerate}
114	  %  Moreover, $\underline{\Spec}_S(\mathcal{A})$
115	  %  is unique up to unique isomorphism over $S$."
116	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], tag 01LQ (lemma-glue-relative-spec);
117	  cf.\ [Hartshorne], II~Ex.~5.17(a).}
118	  Let $X$ be a scheme and $\mathcal{A}$ a quasi-coherent $\mathcal{O}_X$-algebra
119	  (Definition~\ref{def:qc_sheaf_of_algebras}). There exists a scheme
120	  $\underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{A})$ together with an affine morphism
121	  $\pi : \underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{A}) \to X$ such that
122	  \begin{enumerate}
123	    \item for every affine open $U \subseteq X$, there is an isomorphism
124	      $i_U : \pi^{-1}(U) \xrightarrow{\sim} \Spec(\mathcal{A}(U))$ over $U$;
125	    \item for $U \subseteq U' \subseteq X$ both affine open, the composite
126	      $\Spec(\mathcal{A}(U)) \xrightarrow{i_U^{-1}} \pi^{-1}(U) \hookrightarrow
127	      \pi^{-1}(U') \xrightarrow{i_{U'}} \Spec(\mathcal{A}(U'))$
128	      coincides with the open immersion induced by the restriction
129	      $\mathcal{A}(U') \to \mathcal{A}(U)$.
130	  \end{enumerate}
131	  The pair $(\underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{A}), \pi)$ is unique up to unique isomorphism
132	  over $X$.
133	\end{theorem}

230	  Following Stacks tag 01LQ (proof of lemma-spec): the functor $F$ is a Zariski sheaf
231	  because morphisms of schemes and $\mathcal{O}$-algebra maps both glue under open covers.
232	  Choose an affine open cover $X = \bigcup U_i$. The subfunctor
233	  $F_i \subseteq F$ of pairs $(f, \varphi)$ with $f(T) \subseteq U_i$ is representable by
234	  $\Spec(\mathcal{A}(U_i))$ via the affine-base case
235	  (Theorem~\ref{thm:relative_spec_affine_base}) together with the
236	  base-change identification $F_i \cong F_{U_i,\,\mathcal{A}|_{U_i}}$
237	  (Theorem~\ref{thm:relative_spec_base_change}). Each $F_i \hookrightarrow F$ is an open
238	  subfunctor (pull back $U_i$ along $f$). The $F_i$ jointly cover $F$ since the $U_i$
239	  cover $X$. The gluing of representable open subfunctors produces a representing scheme,
240	  which is canonically isomorphic to the glued scheme $\underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{A})$
241	  of Theorem~\ref{thm:relative_spec_exists}.
242	\end{proof}

298	\begin{proof}
299	  \uses{def:qc_sheaf_of_algebras, thm:relative_spec_univ}
300	  % NOTE (iter-176 review): proof closed kernel-clean in Lean, but discharges
301	  % against the placeholder body ``RelativeSpec _𝒜 := X``. The current Lean
302	  % proof is ``change IsAffine (Spec R); inferInstance`` against the canonical
303	  % ``isAffine_Spec`` instance — i.e. it does NOT verify the canonical iso
304	  % ``Spec_X(𝒜) ≅ Spec(Γ(X,𝒜))`` named below. The argument below MUST be
305	  % re-derived in Lean once the genuine relative-spectrum construction lands
306	  % and ``RelativeSpec``'s body upgrades.
307	  Write $X = \Spec R$ and $A = \Gamma(X, \mathcal{A})$. Quasi-coherence of
308	  $\mathcal{A}$ gives $\mathcal{A} = \widetilde{A}$ as $\mathcal{O}_X$-modules
309	  (with the $R$-algebra structure on $A$ inducing the algebra structure on
310	  $\widetilde A$). The ring map $R \to A$ gives a morphism
311	  $f_{\mathrm{univ}} : \Spec A \to \Spec R = X$ and a canonical
312	  $\mathcal{O}_{\Spec A}$-algebra map
313	  $\varphi_{\mathrm{univ}} : f_{\mathrm{univ}}^* \mathcal{A} = \widetilde{A \otimes_R A}
314	  \to \mathcal{O}_{\Spec A} = \widetilde A$ given by multiplication
315	  $a \otimes a' \mapsto aa'$. We verify $(\Spec A, f_{\mathrm{univ}}, \varphi_{\mathrm{univ}})$
316	  represents $F$ on the affine base: given a test scheme $T$ and a pair
317	  $(f, \varphi)$, the composite
318	  $A = \Gamma(X, \mathcal{A}) \xrightarrow{f^\sharp}
319	  \Gamma(T, f^*\mathcal{A}) \xrightarrow{\varphi} \Gamma(T, \mathcal{O}_T)$
320	  is a ring map $A \to \Gamma(T, \mathcal{O}_T)$ and hence corresponds to a morphism
321	  $T \to \Spec A$ by the affine universal property
322	  (Mathlib \texttt{Scheme.toSpec\_naturality} /
323	  \texttt{Scheme.Hom.toSpec}). One checks this assignment inverts the universal map
324	  $(f, \varphi) \mapsto a$ defined by $f = f_{\mathrm{univ}} \circ a$,
325	  $\varphi = a^* \varphi_{\mathrm{univ}}$.
326	\end{proof}
327	
328	\section{Base change}
329	\label{sec:relspec_basechange}
330	
331	\begin{theorem}\leanok
332	[Base change of the relative spectrum]
333	  \label{thm:relative_spec_base_change}
334	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RelativeSpec.base_change}
335	  \uses{def:qc_sheaf_of_algebras, thm:relative_spec_exists}
336	  % NOTE (iter-173 review): the iter-173 file-skeleton encodes this as the
337	  % weaker existential ``∃ 𝒜', Nonempty (pullback ≅ T.RelativeSpec 𝒜')'',
338	  % rather than the canonical iso with a *named* pullback ``g^* 𝒜'' pinned
339	  % by this prose. Iter-174+ factors out ``pullbackQcoh g 𝒜'' and tightens
340	  % the statement to the canonical iso.
341	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], tag 01LS (lemma-spec-base-change and
342	  % lemma-spec-properties part (2))
343	  % (read from references/stacks-constructions.tex, L467-L489 + L662-L691).
344	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
345	  % "In Situation \ref{situation-relative-spec}.
346	  %  Let $F$ be the functor
347	  %  associated to $(S, \mathcal{A})$ above.
348	  %  Let $g : S' \to S$ be a morphism of schemes.
349	  %  Set $\mathcal{A}' = g^*\mathcal{A}$. Let $F'$ be the
350	  %  functor associated to $(S', \mathcal{A}')$ above.
351	  %  Then there is a canonical isomorphism
352	  %  $$
353	  %  F' \cong h_{S'} \times_{h_S} F
354	  %  $$
355	  %  of functors. [...]
356	  %  For every morphism $g : S' \to S$ we have
357	  %  $S' \times_S \underline{\Spec}_S(\mathcal{A}) =
358	  %  \underline{\Spec}_{S'}(g^*\mathcal{A})$."
359	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], tag 01LS (lemma-spec-base-change +
360	  lemma-spec-properties).}
361	  Let $g : T \to X$ be a morphism of schemes and let $\mathcal{A}$ be a quasi-coherent
362	  $\mathcal{O}_X$-algebra. Then $g^* \mathcal{A}$ is a quasi-coherent
363	  $\mathcal{O}_T$-algebra and there is a canonical isomorphism of $T$-schemes
364	  \[
365	    T \times_X \underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{A}) \;\;\cong\;\;
366	    \underline{\Spec}_T(g^* \mathcal{A}).
367	  \]
368	\end{theorem}
369	
370	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "A pair $(f' : T \to S', \varphi' : (f')^*\mathcal{A}' \to \mathcal{O}_T)$
371	% is the same as a pair $(f, \varphi : f^*\mathcal{A} \to \mathcal{O}_T)$
372	% together with a factorization of $f$ as $f = g \circ f'$. Namely with
373	% this notation we have
374	% $(f')^* \mathcal{A}' = (f')^*g^*\mathcal{A} = f^*\mathcal{A}$.
375	% Hence the lemma."
376	\begin{proof}
377	  \uses{thm:relative_spec_univ}
378	  % NOTE (iter-176 review): proof closed kernel-clean in Lean, but discharges
379	  % against the placeholder body ``RelativeSpec _𝒜 := X`` /
380	  % ``structureMorphism _𝒜 := 𝟙 X``. The current Lean proof witnesses the
381	  % existential with the trivial pullback-algebra carrier and the iso
382	  % ``asIso (pullback.fst g (𝟙 X)) : pullback g (𝟙 X) ≅ T`` via
383	  % ``pullback_fst_iso_of_right_iso`` — it does NOT verify the canonical
384	  % base-change iso of relative spectra named below. The Yoneda argument MUST
385	  % be re-derived in Lean once the genuine relative-spectrum construction
386	  % lands and both bodies upgrade.
387	  By Theorem~\ref{thm:relative_spec_univ}, $\underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{A})$ represents
388	  the functor $F$ sending an $X$-scheme $h : S \to X$ to the set of
389	  $\mathcal{O}_S$-algebra maps $h^*\mathcal{A} \to \mathcal{O}_S$. A morphism
390	  $S \to T \times_X \underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{A})$ over $T$ is the same as a pair of an
391	  $X$-morphism $S \to \underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{A})$ together with a $T$-factorisation
392	  of $S \to X$, i.e.\ a morphism $S \to T$ whose composition with $g$ equals
393	  $S \to X$. Tracing through, this is a $T$-morphism $f' : S \to T$ together with an
394	  $\mathcal{O}_S$-algebra map $(f')^*(g^*\mathcal{A}) = f^*\mathcal{A} \to \mathcal{O}_S$.
395	  This is precisely $F'(S)$, the functor for $(T, g^*\mathcal{A})$, so the canonical map
396	  $T \times_X \underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{A}) \to \underline{\Spec}_T(g^*\mathcal{A})$

15	### `\lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.morphismToP1OfGlobalSections}` (chapter: `lem:morphism_to_p1_from_global_sections`)
16	- **Lean target exists**: yes (L204 in the Lean file; `noncomputable def`).
17	- **Signature matches**: **partial** — the Lean takes `(f : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar →+* Γ(X.left, ⊤))`, `_halg` (a concrete `kbar`-algebra-map equation), and `_hf` (irrelevant-ideal → unit ideal). The blueprint statement frames the lemma as "two global sections `s₀, s₁ ∈ H⁰(X, ℒ)` of an invertible sheaf without common zeros → unique morphism `φ : X → ℙ¹` with `φ*𝒪(1) = ℒ` and `φ*xᵢ = sᵢ`". The Lean specialises the input shape to the algebra-hom packaging consumed by `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections` (so implicitly `ℒ = 𝒪_X` with sections embedded into `Γ(X, ⊤)`), and adds the `_halg` `kbar`-algebra-compatibility hypothesis that the chapter never mentions. The Lean conclusion is the morphism in `Over (Spec kbar)`; the blueprint claims existence + uniqueness + line-bundle-pullback + section-pullback identities, none of which are output by the Lean wrapper (it returns only the morphism, with no uniqueness/pullback bundling).
18	- **Proof follows sketch**: **partial** — the Lean body genuinely invokes `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) f _hf` and closes the slice-category section condition via `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections_toSpecZero`, `Scheme.toSpecΓ_naturality`, and `toSpecΓ_SpecMap_ΓSpecIso_inv` (a substantive ~20-line tactic chain that uses `_halg` to bridge `f.comp MvPolynomial.C` to `(ΓSpecIso _).inv ≫ X.hom.appTop`). This matches the chapter's high-level "specialise `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections` to `ℕ`-graded `k̄[x₀, x₁]`" strategy. **However**, the chapter's proof sketch additionally claims the wrapper "chains `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections` with the chart-restriction-and-glue pattern of `Scheme.Cover.glueMorphisms`" (L150-154). The actual Lean body uses neither `glueMorphisms` nor any chart cover — the chart-glue reduction (`ℒ`-sections → algebra-hom into `Γ(X, ⊤)`) is absorbed into the **caller's** responsibility to construct `f`. The blueprint prose overstates what this wrapper does.

Signature now correctly threads `_halg` — the iter-178 audit gap (audited on the OLD signature without `_halg`) is resolved on the Lean side, but the chapter prose has not been updated to mention `_halg`, the slice-category section-condition obligation, or the fact that the chart-glue is upstream rather than inside this wrapper.

Pin 1's chapter proof (L118-162) explains the Mathlib `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections` invocation and discusses chart-glue, but does not preview either of the two substantive ingredients of the *actually closed* Lean body:
59	  1. The `_halg` algebra-compatibility hypothesis (without which the slice-category section condition over `Spec kbar` cannot be derived). The Lean docstring (L186-188) is explicit: "`halg` is the `kbar`-algebra-compatibility condition (without which the section condition over `Spec kbar` cannot be derived)". The chapter is silent on this obligation.
60	  2. The slice-category lift via `Over.homMk` and the section-condition proof that uses `Scheme.toSpecΓ_naturality` + `toSpecΓ_SpecMap_ΓSpecIso_inv` + `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections_toSpecZero`. The chapter's proof talks about "Mathlib's `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections` lands in `Proj 𝒜 = ℙ¹`" but skips the lift to `Over (Spec k̄)`.

547	\cref{def:order_at_point} and \cref{def:principal_divisor}.
548	
549	\begin{theorem}
550	
551	\leanok
552	  [Weil-divisor characterisation of the codim-1 extension obstruction]
553	  \label{thm:weil_divisor_obstruction}
554	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.mem_domain_iff_exists_partialMap_through_point}
555	  \uses{def:order_at_point, def:codim1_cycles, def:indeterminacy_locus,
556	        lem:smooth_codim_one_dvr}
557	  % NOTE (iter-179 review): the substantive Hartshorne-II.6 valuative
558	  % reformulation (the iff with ord_W >= 0) is deferred to a planned future
559	  % lemma `extend_iff_pullback_order_nonneg` carrying
560	  % `Scheme.RationalMap.order` machinery that does not yet ship in Mathlib
561	  % at the pinned commit b80f227. The current Lean target proves the
562	  % lighter mem_domain reshuffle ("eta_W in Dom(f) iff there is a partial
563	  % map representative through eta_W"). See task_results/Albanese_CodimOneExtension.lean.md
564	  % iter-179 Lane D Path D2 + this chapter pin's downstream-recommendation
565	  % notes for the iter-180+ upgrade trigger.
566	  % SOURCE: [Hartshorne], II.6, pp.~130--131 (the valuation $v_Y$ associated to a
567	  % prime divisor, and the characterisation of $\mathcal O_{X, \eta_Y}$ as the
568	  % subring of $K(X)$ on which $v_Y \geq 0$) (read from
569	  % references/hartshorne-algebraic-geometry.pdf, PDF pages 147--148).
570	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "If $Y$ is a prime divisor on $X$, let $\eta \in Y$ be its generic
571	  % point. Then the local ring $\mathcal O_{\eta, X}$ of $X$ at $\eta$ is a discrete
572	  % valuation ring with quotient field $K$, the function field of $X$. We call the
573	  % corresponding discrete valuation $v_Y$ the \emph{valuation of $Y$}."
574	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Now let $f \in K^\ast$ be any nonzero rational function on $X$.
575	  % Then $v_Y(f)$ is an integer. If it is positive, we say $f$ has a \emph{zero} along
576	  % $Y$, of that order; if it is negative, we say $f$ has a \emph{pole} along $Y$, of
577	  % order $-v_Y(f)$."
578	  \textit{Source: Hartshorne, II.6, pp.~130--131 (valuation $v_Y$ and the order map).}
579	  Let $X$ be a nonsingular variety over $\bar k$, let $Y$ be a variety over $\bar k$,
580	  let $f \colon X \dashrightarrow Y$ be a rational map, and let $W \subseteq X$ be a
581	  prime divisor with generic point $\eta_W \in W$ (so
582	  $\codim_X \overline{\{\eta_W\}} = 1$). Let $(U, \varphi_U)$ be any representative of
583	  $f$ with $U \cap (X \smallsetminus W) \neq \emptyset$. The following are equivalent:
584	  \begin{enumerate}
585	    \item $f$ is defined at $\eta_W$ (i.e.\ $\eta_W \in \mathrm{Dom}(f)$, equivalently
586	      $W \not\subseteq Z(f)$).
587	    \item For some (equivalently, every) affine open neighbourhood $V \subseteq Y$ of
588	      the generic image of $f$ near $W$, every regular function $g \in
589	      \mathcal O_Y(V)$ pulls back to a rational function $\varphi_U^\ast(g) \in K(X)$
590	      satisfying
591	      \[
592	        \ord_W\bigl(\varphi_U^\ast(g)\bigr) \;\geq\; 0.
593	      \]
594	  \end{enumerate}
595	
596	  Consequently: $f$ is codim-$1$-indeterminacy-free
597	  (\cref{def:codim_one_indeterminacy}) if and only if, for every prime divisor
598	  $W \subseteq X$ and every affine open $V \subseteq Y$ as above, the pullback
599	  $\varphi_U^\ast(g)$ has $\ord_W \geq 0$ for all $g \in \mathcal O_Y(V)$.
600	\end{theorem}
601	
602	\begin{proof}
603	  \uses{def:order_at_point, def:codim1_cycles, lem:smooth_codim_one_dvr,
604	        thm:codim_one_extension}
605	  \leanok
606	  By \cref{lem:smooth_codim_one_dvr}, the local ring $\mathcal O_{X, \eta_W}$ is a
607	  DVR with fraction field $K(X)$ and uniformiser determined by $W$; by
608	  \cref{def:order_at_point} its valuation is exactly $\ord_W$. The local ring at
609	  $\eta_W$ is therefore characterised inside $K(X)$ as the subring
610	  \[
611	    \mathcal O_{X, \eta_W} \;=\; \bigl\{\, h \in K(X) \;\big|\; \ord_W(h) \geq 0
612	    \,\bigr\} \cup \{0\}.
613	  \]
614	  The rational map $f$ is defined at $\eta_W$ if and only if, for any affine open
615	  $V = \operatorname{Spec} A \subseteq Y$ around the image $\overline{f(\eta_W)}$,
616	  the pullback $\varphi_U^\ast \colon A \to K(X)$ lands in
617	  $\mathcal O_{X, \eta_W}$: this is the standard local characterisation of where a
618	  rational map is regular at a codim-$1$ point of a smooth variety (it is the
619	  ``regular = no pole'' criterion). By the displayed identity, the latter is
620	  equivalent to $\ord_W(\varphi_U^\ast(g)) \geq 0$ for every $g \in A$, which is
621	  condition (2). The equivalence (1)~$\Leftrightarrow$~(2) is therefore established.
622	
623	  For the consequence, $f$ is codim-$1$-indeterminacy-free iff no prime divisor $W$ is
624	  contained in $Z(f)$, which by the equivalence above is iff the $\ord_W \geq 0$
625	  condition holds for every prime divisor $W$.
626	\end{proof}
627	
628	\begin{remark}
629	  \label{rmk:weil_obstruction_application}
630	  \textbf{Why this form is the prover-facing one.} In the consumer chapter
631	  \cref{chap:Albanese_Thm32RationalMapExtension} the Lean prover combines
632	  \cref{thm:codim_one_extension} with \cref{lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy}; in
633	  isolation, the abstract codim-$\geq 2$ statement

The blueprint pins the depth as an `(Ideal R)`-indexed function returning a value in `{0,1,…,∞}`; the Lean choice of `ℕ∞` is standard. `_I` / `_M` underscore-prefixing is acceptable because the body is a typed `sorry`.

Chapter prose pins `depth(M) = inf{i : Ext^i(κ, M) ≠ 0}`; Lean encodes the equivalent depth-bound `↔` `Ext`-vanishing-below `(n ≤ depth(M)) ↔ (∀ i < n, Ext^i(κ, M) = 0)`. The two are logically equivalent and the docstring (L213-224) explicitly explains the reformulation choice as an "inductive-proof-convenient form".

The short-exact-sequence hypothesis is encoded by `f : N' →ₗ[R] N`, `g : N →ₗ[R] N''`, `Function.Injective f`, `Function.Surjective g`, `Function.Exact f g`, which is the standard Mathlib idiom.

The L374-392 docstring scopes the iter-176+ body to either the direct regular-sequence argument (Stacks 00OD direct proof) or the Auslander–Buchsbaum-applied-to-`R` route, matching the chapter's `\begin{proof}` (L451-475) which gives the direct argument plus a "Remark on the Auslander–Buchsbaum route" elaboration.

Each `\lean{...}` pin names a concrete Lean identifier that exists in the file at the expected namespace, with no ambiguity between `RingTheory.Module.depth` vs `Module.depth` etc. The chapter even threads the namespace discipline: `\lean{Module.projectiveDimension}` (NOT `RingTheory.Module.projectiveDimension`) matches the namespace choice at L165-187 (Mathlib-convention `Module` namespace for the re-exported categorical projective dimension, as opposed to `RingTheory.Module` for the depth function which has no Mathlib counterpart at this commit).

1	# Lean ↔ Blueprint Check Report

11	- Blueprint: `blueprint/src/chapters/Albanese_Thm32RationalMapExtension.tex`
12	
13	## Per-declaration
14	
15	### `\lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.extend_to_av}` (chapter: `thm:rational_map_to_av_extends`)
16	- **Lean target exists**: yes — `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.extend_to_av` at line 239.
17	- **Signature matches**: yes.
18	  - Blueprint statement (§ Setup, Theorem `thm:rational_map_to_av_extends`):
19	    "Let $X$ be a nonsingular variety over $\bar k$, $A$ an abelian variety
20	    over $\bar k$, and $f \colon X \dashrightarrow A$. Then there is a unique
21	    regular morphism $\widetilde f \colon X \to A$ whose restriction to a
22	    dense open of definition agrees with $f$."
23	  - Lean conclusion: `∃! (g : X.left ⟶ A.left), g.toRationalMap = f`.
24	  - The instance bundle on `X` (smooth, geometrically irreducible,
25	    separated, locally of finite type, integral, reduced) matches the
26	    "nonsingular variety over $\bar k$" convention in the module docstring,
27	    which itself matches the blueprint's "nonsingular variety" definition
28	    (§ Setup, "geometrically integral, geometrically reduced, separated
29	    scheme of finite type over $\bar k$ that is moreover smooth").
30	  - The instance bundle on `A` (`[GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom]
31	    [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]`) matches the "abelian variety"
32	    convention used elsewhere in the project (cf.
33	    `AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean`, `Jacobian.lean`).
34	  - Both `kbar` universe and `IsAlgClosed` match the blueprint's "algebraically
35	    closed field $\bar k$".
36	- **Proof follows sketch**: partial.
37	  - Blueprint proof sketch (§ Setup, proof of `thm:rational_map_to_av_extends`):
38	    "Combine Theorem 3.1 with Lemma 3.3. Concretely: let $U \subseteq X$ be
39	    maximal of definition, $Z = X \setminus U$. (1) by `thm:codim_one_extension`,
40	    $\mathrm{codim}_X(Z) \geq 2$. (2) by `lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy`,
41	    $Z$ is empty or pure codim 1. (3) both force $Z = \emptyset$, hence
42	    $f$ admits a regular representative on all of $X$. Uniqueness via
43	    reduced-and-separated agreement."
44	  - Lean body structure: derive `IsSeparated A.hom` / `LocallyOfFiniteType
45	    A.hom` by `inferInstance`; extract `⟨IsIntegral A.left, CodimOneFree f⟩`
46	    from the private helper `av_isIntegral_and_codimOneFree`; derive
47	    `IsReduced A.left` by `inferInstance`; apply
48	    `extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth f hcod` (project-side Milne 3.1
49	    specialised to a `CodimOneFree`-input form).
50	  - **Mathematical match**: the combination Lemma 3.3 + codim-≥2 half of
51	    Theorem 3.1 → `Z = ∅` is consolidated into the private helper
52	    `av_isIntegral_and_codimOneFree` (whose docstring at L176–179 spells out
53	    the exact two-step argument the blueprint enumerates). The remaining
54	    invocation of `extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth` is then literally
55	    "Theorem 3.1, given that the codim-1 indeterminacy obstruction is
56	    discharged". The Lean body's overall mathematical content thus matches
57	    the blueprint sketch step-for-step.
58	  - **Why partial**: the helper `av_isIntegral_and_codimOneFree` is
59	    `sorry`-bodied (directive § Known issues item 1). The proof body of
60	    `extend_to_av` is therefore complete in *structure* (no inline `sorry`)
61	    but the substantive Lemma-3.3-plus-codim-≥2 combination it consumes is
62	    not yet discharged. This is acknowledged in the directive's "Known
63	    issues" block and in the helper docstring at L181–186.
64	- **notes**:
65	  - The blueprint sketch is written as "Theorem 3.1 gives codim ≥ 2" and
66	    "Lemma 3.3 gives empty-or-pure-codim-1". The Lean's
67	    `extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth` is Theorem 3.1 *specialised to a
68	    `CodimOneFree` input* — i.e. the project has split Theorem 3.1 into
69	    "produces codim ≥ 2 at the indeterminacy locus" + "given codim-1
70	    indeterminacy-free, extends". Item (1) of the blueprint sketch is the
71	    half of Theorem 3.1 the helper consolidates; item (3) is the wrap-up;
72	    the literal Lean call `extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth f hcod` is the
73	    *second* half of Theorem 3.1 turned into the actual existence statement.
74	    This split is documented in `Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean`'s header
75	    and in the helper's docstring; no signature departure.
76	  - Uniqueness (`∃!` rather than `∃`) is handled inside
77	    `extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth`, which itself returns `∃!`.
78	    Consistent with the blueprint's "uniqueness via reduced-and-separated
79	    agreement principle" remark.
80	
81	## Red flags
82	
83	### Placeholder / suspect bodies
84	- `av_isIntegral_and_codimOneFree` (L187–198) — private helper, body
85	  `:= sorry`. The helper packages exactly the two known pieces the
86	  directive identifies: (i) `IsIntegral A.left` (Mathlib `Smooth ⟹
87	  IsReduced` bridge gap), and (ii) `CodimOneFree f` (Lemma 3.3 + codim-≥2
88	  half of Theorem 3.1 not unbundled from `extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth`).
89	  Acknowledged in the directive § Known issues — **not re-flagged as
90	  must-fix-this-iter**; documented status flag only.
91	
92	### Excuse-comments
93	- None. The module docstring's "iter-175 Lane J file-skeleton" / "iter-179
94	  Lane E body" status notes describe progress without excusing wrong
95	  content. The helper's docstring documents the residual `sorry` honestly
96	  with the exact two missing inputs.
97	
98	### Axioms / Classical.choice on non-trivial claims
99	- None.
100	
101	## Unreferenced declarations (informational)
102	
103	- `av_isIntegral_and_codimOneFree` (L187, `private theorem`). Not
104	  blueprint-pinned. Acceptable as a helper *if* the iter-179 Lane E plan is
105	  to keep its `sorry` localised here. **Comment for downstream**: once the
106	  two missing inputs land (Mathlib `Smooth ⟹ IsReduced` bridge + a
107	  standalone `codim ≥ 2 at indeterminacy of map to a complete variety`
108	  lemma exported from `Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean`), this helper
109	  should close axiom-clean by inlining the two derivations; at that point
110	  it can either remain `private` (current behaviour) or be promoted to a
111	  blueprint-pinned lemma alongside `extend_to_av`.
112	
113	## Blueprint adequacy for this file
114	
115	- **Coverage**: 1/1 blueprint-pinned Lean declaration accounted for. 1
116	  helper (`av_isIntegral_and_codimOneFree`) is unpinned and acceptable as
117	  helper-only (a `sorry`-consolidator that will be inlined once its two
118	  sub-pieces land upstream).
119	- **Proof-sketch depth**: adequate. The three-step enumerate in the
120	  blueprint (L86–97) pins the exact two inputs (`thm:codim_one_extension`
121	  + `lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy`) and the exact combination ("$Z$ has
122	  codim $\geq 2$ and is empty-or-pure-codim-1, hence $Z = \emptyset$").
123	  The Lean body's structure matches.
124	- **Hint precision**: precise. `\lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.extend_to_av}`
125	  pins the exact target; namespace housing
126	  (`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap`) matches the Mathlib idiom the
127	  blueprint § Lean encoding (L182–206) recommends.
128	- **Generality**: matches need. The blueprint's nonsingular-variety and
129	  abelian-variety conventions (§ Setup) match the Lean's instance bundles
130	  one-for-one.
131	- **Minor adequacy gap (informational, not blocking)**: The blueprint
132	  proof sketch does not mention that on the Lean side the abelian-variety
133	  convention does NOT carry `IsIntegral A.left` / `IsSeparated A.hom` /
134	  `LocallyOfFiniteType A.hom` / `IsReduced A.left` automatically — the
135	  prover had to materialise these to feed
136	  `extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth`. The Lean ended up writing a 5-line
137	  instance-resolution preamble (L251–255) plus a `sorry`-bodied helper
138	  carrying `IsIntegral A.left` because the `Smooth ⟹ IsReduced` Mathlib
139	  bridge is missing at the pinned commit. The blueprint's § Lean encoding
140	  (L182–206) currently says "the proof body imports … in the three-step
141	  pattern above"; a one-line addition flagging the instance-resolution
142	  preamble (and the `IsIntegral A.left` Mathlib gap as a transient
143	  obstacle) would make the chapter exactly match what a prover faces in
144	  practice.
145	- **Recommended chapter-side actions**:
146	  - (Minor, optional) Append a one-line note to § Lean encoding (around
147	    L207–211) of the form: "On the Lean side, the abelian-variety
148	    convention `[GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom]
149	    [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]` does not directly synthesise
150	    `IsIntegral A.left` / `IsReduced A.left` / `IsSeparated A.hom` /
151	    `LocallyOfFiniteType A.hom`; the proof body materialises these by
152	    `inferInstance` (with the `IsIntegral A.left` step transiently routed
153	    through a `sorry`-bodied helper until the Mathlib `Smooth ⟹ IsReduced`
154	    bridge lands)." This is housekeeping, not a gating issue.
155	
156	## Severity summary
157	
158	- **must-fix-this-iter**: none.
159	  - The helper `av_isIntegral_and_codimOneFree`'s `sorry` is declared a
160	    known issue in the directive and is not classified as must-fix
161	    per the directive's "Known issues" channel.
162	  - `extend_to_av` itself has a substantive (non-placeholder) proof body
163	    that structurally matches the blueprint sketch.
164	- **major**: none.
165	- **minor**:
166	  - Blueprint § Lean encoding could note the instance-resolution preamble
167	    + the `Smooth ⟹ IsReduced` Mathlib gap (housekeeping; not blocking).
168	
169	Overall verdict: `extend_to_av`'s landed body matches the blueprint's
170	three-step combine-3.1-with-3.3 sketch in mathematical content and
171	structure; the residual `sorry` lives in the private helper documented in
